description: The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company, together with its subsidiaries, develops, manufactures, distributes, and sells tires and related products and services worldwide. It offers various lines of tires for automobiles, trucks, buses, aircraft, motorcycles, earthmoving equipment, and mining and industrial equipment under the Goodyear, Cooper, Dunlop, Kelly, Debica, Sava, Fulda, Mastercraft, Roadmaster, and various other house brands, as well as under the private-label brands. The company also retreads truck, aviation, and off-the-road tires; manufactures and sells tread rubber and other tire retreading materials; sells chemical and natural rubber products; and provides automotive and commercial truck maintenance and repair services, and miscellaneous other products and services. It operates approximately 1,000 retail outlets, which offer products for retail sale, and provides repair and other services. The company sells its products worldwide through a network of independent dealers, regional distributors, retail outlets, and retailers. The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company was incorporated in 1898 and is headquartered in Akron, Ohio.
ai_description: The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company manufactures and sells tires and related products and services for automobiles, light trucks, commercial trucks and trailers, aviation, motorcycles, racing cars, farm equipment, and heavy earth-moving machinery; it offers tire brands including Goodyear, Cooper, Kelly, Mastercraft, Mickey Thompson, and Roadmaster, as well as tire retreading, automotive maintenance programs, digital fleet monitoring platforms, and tire-related technologies such as run-flat and all-season designs. Founded in 1898 and headquartered in Akron, Ohio, the company operates manufacturing facilities in 21 countries across North America, Europe, Asia, Latin America, and other regions, serving original equipment manufacturers, replacement markets, fleet operators, and consumers worldwide. In 2025, Goodyear completed the sale of its off-the-road tire business to Yokohama Rubber, the Dunlop brand, and its chemical business for total gross proceeds of approximately $2.2 billion to reduce debt under the Goodyear Forward transformation plan; it also invests $1 billion in global facility modernization, expands premium tire capacity by 10 million units, advances electric vehicle tire technologies like EQMAX with sustainable materials, and targets $1.5 billion in annual run-rate cost savings and 10% segment operating margins by year-end.
